records.ety: [L. depravare, depravatum; de- + pravus crooked, distorted, perverse, wicked.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Depraved (d*prāvd"); p. pr. & vb. n. Depraving.]

defs: To speak ill of; to depreciate; to malign; to revile.
quotes: And thou knowest, conscience, I came not to chide Nor deprave thy person with a proud heart.

defs: To make bad or worse; to vitiate; to corrupt.
quotes: Whose pride depraves each other better part.
